rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRIGIDA JUBILO CUSTODIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Court-Requested Brief Re Motion For Determination That Creditors Claim And Action Thereon Filed By Creditors Michele Baker And David Banyard Is Time-Barred As To Decedent Brigida Jubilo Custodia And The Estate
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner's Motion for Court Determination of Timeliness of Creditor's Claim is granted. The Claimant's Creditor's Claim, filed on July 3, 2017, for $197,835.95, is time barred under CCP § 366.2. It appears from the papers that Claimants are actually seeking to enforce an existing judicial lien under Probate Code § 9391. However, the Claimants do not appear to have brought an action to foreclose the lien against the judgment debtor's property under Probate Code § 9391, and have not expressly waived all recourse against other property in the estate. The time in which to bring an action to enforce a lien is not controlled by CCP § 366.2, although statutes of limitation otherwise applicable to an action to enforce a lien continue to apply. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, January 30, 2018.
